metadata.description: Compliance signal library extracted from Industry Guidances documents. Used to flag non-compliant Base44 web apps.

signals.signal_name: Selling prescription drugs without requiring a valid prescription
signals.description: Any site selling prescription-only drugs without requiring an in-person physician examination and valid prescription is operating illegally in virtually every jurisdiction.
Internet pharmacies offering worldwide or multi-country shipping of prescription drugs cannot possibly be legally compliant, as they would need a pharmacy license in every destination jurisdiction.
Drugs not approved by the FDA or other competent authority, or knockoffs of approved drugs (e.g., Kamagra, Silagra, Filagra, Careprost, Retino-A), sold outside authorized supply chains.
Sites offering prescriptions solely based on filling out an online form, without a prior in-person doctor examination, are operating illegally in almost all jurisdictions.
phrases: fill out a short form to get your medication

signals.signal_name: Rx-only drugs sold as OTC or cosmetics
signals.description: Prescription-only drugs being marketed or sold without prescription requirements, including: sildenafil, tadalafil, vardenafil, semaglutide, tretinoin, clobetasol, bimatoprost, betamethasone, hydroquinone, clindamycin, ketoconazole, minoxidil >5%, orlistat 120mg, pantoprazole, dexamethasone, fluocinolone, triamcinolone (topical), acyclovir, azelaic acid ≥15%, ciclopirox, eflornithine, tazarotene, cromoglicate sodium eye drops, cyproheptadine, sertaconazole, methocarbamol, ibuprofen ≥400mg.
DMAA is an amphetamine derivative not approved for any medical use. FDA considers it an unsafe food additive. Multiple warning letters issued.
Contains amphetamine-like chemicals. FDA considers products containing Acacia rigidula to be adulterated. New dietary ingredient not meeting conditions for supplements.
Produces industrial bleach (chlorine dioxide) when ingested. Falsely marketed to cure cancer, HIV/AIDS, autism. FDA warns of serious harm.
Pure or highly concentrated caffeine powder/liquid is considered adulterated under FDCA. Linked to deaths.

signals.signal_name: SARMs (Ostarine / GW-501516 / Cardarine) and anabolic steroids
signals.description: SARMs (e.g., Ostarine/MK-2866, GW-501516/Cardarine/Endurobol) and anabolic steroids (Anadrol, Anavar, Deca-Durbolin, Dianabol, Testosterone, Trenbolone, Winstrol) are not lawfully sold as dietary supplements. Associated with liver toxicity, heart attack, stroke.
Synthetic combination of niacin and GABA. FDA states any dietary supplements containing picamilon are misbranded as it does not meet the statutory definition of a dietary ingredient.
Yohimbine is a prescription-only drug in AU, CA, DE, HK, IE, ES, UK, JP, SG, KR, NL. Even where legal, supplements cannot make disease claims about it.
Dietary supplements making disease claims are treated as unapproved drugs by the FDA. Claims to diagnose, cure, mitigate, treat, or prevent any disease are prohibited.
phrases: protective against the development of cancer
reduces the pain and stiffness associated with arthritis
supports the body's ability to resist infection
protects from the damaging effects of chemotherapy

signals.description: Claiming a supplement is a substitute for a drug, or uses drug class terminology, constitutes an implied disease claim.
phrases: safe and effective alternative to Adderall
as close as you can get to taking Viagra

signals.signal_name: Disease name in product name or meta tags
signals.description: Using disease names (e.g., diabetes, cancer, arthritis) in product names, URLs, or meta tags constitutes a disease claim.

signals.signal_name: Foreign topical drugs marketed as cosmetics (whitening / anti-wrinkle / eyelash)
signals.description: Foreign prescription-only topical drugs containing clobetasol, betamethasone, tretinoin, or bimatoprost marketed as cosmetics (whitening creams, anti-wrinkle creams, eyelash serums) are illegal in the US and trigger BRAM/GBPP violations.
Cosmetics making claims to affect the structure or function of the body, or treat diseases, become unapproved drugs.
keywords: stimulates collagen production at cellular level

signals.description: Kratom (Mitragyna speciosa) is a DEA Drug of Concern, subject to FDA import alerts, and controlled in numerous countries and US states. Violates major card brand rules (GBPP).
signals.regulation: DEA Drug of Concern; FDA import alert; GBPP; country-specific controls

signals.description: Controlled or restricted psychoactive substances including magic mushrooms (psilocybin), peyote, mescaline-containing cacti, DMT-containing plants, ayahuasca, iboga, Salvia divinorum, GHB, synthetic cannabinoids, synthetic cathinones, synthetic opioids.
phrases: research chemicals not for human consumption
signals.regulation: DEA Schedule I/II; CSA; FDCA; GBPP; country-specific controls

signals.description: Psychoactive products are often disguised with benign labels. Common disguise patterns include: 'not for human consumption', 'for research purposes only', 'plant food', 'bath salts', 'jewelry cleaner', 'leather cleaner', 'room deodoriser', 'untreated/unwashed seeds', poppy pods sold as 'dried flowers' or 'craft supplies'.

signals.signal_name: Drug paraphernalia - items for use with illicit drugs
signals.description: Under US Code Title 21 Section 863, it is unlawful to sell or offer for sale drug paraphernalia. Includes bongs, pipes, roach clips, chillums, miniature spoons, cocaine freebase kits, water pipes.

signals.signal_name: E-cigarettes, vaping devices, and ENDS components
signals.description: E-cigarettes and all ENDS components (e-liquids, cartridges, atomizers, batteries, vape juice) are FDA-regulated tobacco products. Many PSPs prohibit card-not-present e-cigarette merchants. High risk for selling to minors and card brand violations.
signals.regulation: FDCA (ENDS); FDA Final Rule 2016; PACT Act; GBPP

signals.signal_name: Vape/CBD products with disease treatment claims
signals.description: Vape or CBD products marketed with claims to treat or cure diseases (e.g., anxiety, pain, cancer) are subject to FDA enforcement action.

signals.signal_name: Classic transaction laundering / bank page indicators
signals.description: Transaction laundering occurs when an illicit merchant obtains a merchant account for a seemingly innocuous business and uses it to process payments for prohibited products/services. Red flags: generic template website, sparse product catalog, illogical pricing, lack of social media presence, incongruous products.
signals.regulation: BRAM; GBPP; Mastercard rules; Visa rules; BSA/AML

signals.signal_name: IPTV / streaming services as transaction laundering front
signals.description: Illicit IPTV merchants often use web hosting or VPS services as laundering fronts. Common pattern: subscription to 'web hosting' that actually processes for IPTV piracy.

signals.signal_name: Negative-option / continuity billing without clear disclosure
signals.description: Merchants enrolling customers in recurring subscriptions via free trials without clear disclosure of terms or easy cancellation. Regulated by FTC, ROSCA, and Mastercard (MCC 5968).
phrases: you will be charged after trial
signals.regulation: FTC Act; ROSCA (15 USC § 8403); Mastercard standards; MCC 5968

signals.signal_name: Fake job opportunities and resume repair scams
signals.description: Services promising job placement or resume repair with fake guarantees, upfront fees, and false placement rates.

signals.signal_name: Fake emotional support / assistance animal documentation
signals.description: Merchants selling sham ESA/service animal certificates, registrations, or ID cards. These documents convey no legal rights and are not recognized by DOJ or HUD.

signals.signal_name: Government form / application resellers charging excessive fees
signals.description: Merchants charging fees to facilitate government applications available for free or low cost directly: ESTA travel authorization, DMV services, fishing/hunting licenses, immigration services, visa processing, birth/marriage certificates.

Selling counterfeit, replica, or unauthorized goods using branded designs/logos. High-risk brands: Louis Vuitton, Gucci, Nike, Adidas, Disney, Star Wars, Adobe, Microsoft. Triggers BRAM and GBPP fines.
IPTV services offering premium/pay-per-view channels, large channel counts, or access to copyrighted content for suspiciously low fees. Violates Visa and Mastercard rules on copyright infringement.
File-sharing services potentially hiding copyrighted material behind paywalls. Risk indicator: marketing cyberlocker as means to share/distribute copyrighted content.

signals.signal_name: Crypto scams, rug pulls, and pump-and-dump schemes
signals.description: Fraudulent cryptocurrency projects, fake wallets, pump-and-dump schemes, and rug pulls solicited via social media.
signals.regulation: FTC Act; SEC regulations; wire fraud statutes

signals.description: Fraudulent fundraising campaigns impersonating legitimate charities or aid organizations, especially exploiting disasters/pandemics.
Credit Privacy Numbers (CPNs) are fraudulently marketed as legal replacements for SSNs to avoid bad credit history. This is a federal crime.
source_files: CPNs_ A Crime, Not a Clean Slate.pdf

signals.signal_name: CBD products with disease claims or interstate shipping complications
signals.description: CBD exists in a complex legal environment. FDA has not approved CBD for use in food/supplements in the US. CBD marketed with disease claims is subject to FDA enforcement. Some US states prohibit CBD sales. In the EU, CBD is classified as a Novel Food requiring pre-market authorization.
source_files: US State CBD Laws You Need to Know About.pdf
CBD Is a Novel Food EU.docx
signals.regulation: FDCA; FDA enforcement; EU Novel Food Regulation; state laws

signals.description: Delta-8 THC is prohibited in foods and dietary supplements under FDCA and is explicitly prohibited in several US states. THCO is classified as a Schedule I controlled substance by DEA.

Online gambling is illegal in many US states and jurisdictions. Merchants are often blocked from accepting credit cards for gambling. E-voucher and cryptocurrency routing are common laundering tactics.
signals.regulation: UIGEA; state gambling laws; BRAM; GBPP

signals.description: Content promoting hatred, hostility, or violence towards protected groups based on race, religion, ethnicity, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, disability, or other characteristics. Significant reputational risk and potential BRAM/GBPP violations.
signals.regulation: BRAM; GBPP; platform policies; hate crime laws

signals.description: Products falsely claiming to prevent, treat, or cure COVID-19. US sellers making 'FDA Cleared' claims for PPE must ensure accurate certification. EU sellers must ensure CE certification for medical devices.
Illicit operators use geo-targeting/cloaking to show legitimate content to investigators while serving illegal content to real users. Typosquatting involves domain names similar to legitimate sites.
Sale of protected wildlife, wildlife parts, or products derived from endangered species. Linked to money laundering and organized crime per FATF.
signals.regulation: CITES; Lacey Act; FATF recommendations; BRAM; GBPP

signals.description: High-pressure sales tactics, implausibly low prices, celebrity endorsements, and urgency language are common across deceptive merchants.
phrases: act now before it's too late

signals.description: Sale of 80% lower receivers, ghost gun kits, unfinished frames/receivers, or 3D-printed firearm files that circumvent background checks and registration.
signals.regulation: Gun Control Act; state firearm laws; ATF regulations
